method_atom: A duality that maps a strongly-coupled many-body system onto an equivalent higher-dimensional curved geometry with a thermal horizon, where the interaction energy between two point sources on the boundary is computed as the regularized (background-subtracted) action of a minimal-area extended object anchored to those sources. By analytically continuing the extremal configuration into the complex plane and adopting a modified subtraction, one obtains a smooth complex-valued interaction energy whose non-zero imaginary part signals absorption/dissipation and whose real part decays as an inverse power law rather than exponentially at large separation.
object_atom: A heavy quark and anti-quark pair immersed in a hot, strongly-coupled N=4 supersymmetric Yang-Mills plasma at finite temperature, whose binding potential is being computed.
brief_reason: The dual-geometry / minimal-surface plus complex-continuation and subtraction machinery for extracting a source-source interaction energy is transferable, while the heavy quark-antiquark pair in a thermal gauge-theory plasma is the physics-specific object; the two are separable, though the specific horizon geometry ties the machinery closely to the thermal setting.
